differential_diagnoses: Duodenal perforation (penetrating peptic ulcer) with secondary retroperitoneal abscess and septic thrombophlebitis
Streptococcus anginosus group invasive infection (liver abscess/retroperitoneal abscess)
next_steps: Urgent surgical or interventional radiology consultation for retroperitoneal abscess drainage
Transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
High-resolution CT abdomen with oral contrast (or repeat MRI) to better characterize the duodenal-renal interface
Continued targeted antibiotic therapy (e.g., ceftriaxone) based on culture sensitivities
Maintain anticoagulation and monitor for propagation of venous thrombus
Upper endoscopy to evaluate for duodenal pathology if stable

differential_diagnoses: Penetrating duodenal ulcer with secondary retroperitoneal abscess and septic thrombophlebitis
Streptococcus anginosus group invasive infection (liver phlegmon/retroperitoneal abscess)
Secondary antiphospholipid syndrome due to infection/inflammation
Lemierre-like syndrome arising from gastrointestinal source
next_steps: Repeat abdominal CT with oral contrast to re-evaluate the duodenal-renal interface
Stool examination and serology for Strongyloides stercoralis
Continued parenteral antibiotics with adjustment based on clinical response and source control
Interventional radiology consultation for potential percutaneous drainage of retroperitoneal fluid collection
Continued therapeutic anticoagulation with close monitoring for bleeding
